input_row.title: Ensure public network access is disabled
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services or Function App. 2. Click the name of a function app. 3. Under Settings, click Networking. 4. Under Inbound traffic configuration, click the text next to Public network access. 5. Select the radio button next to Disabled. 6. Click Save. 7. Check the box to confirm the change. 8. Click Continue. 9. Repeat steps 1-8 for each function app requiring remediation. Remediate from Azure CLI For each function app requiring remediation, run the following command to disable public network access: az resource update --resource-group <resource-group-name> --name <function- app-name> --resource-type "Microsoft.Web/sites" --set properties.publicNetworkAccess=Disabled Default Value: By default, public network access is enabled. References: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/app-service/networking-features 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/functionapp 3. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/resource CIS Controls:
input_row.reference: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/app-service/networking-features 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/functionapp 3. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/resource CIS Controls: Controls Version Control IG 1 IG 2 IG 3 v8 3.3 Configure Data Access Control Lists Configure data access control lists based on a user’s need to know. Apply data access control lists, also known as access permissions, to local and remote file systems, databases, and applications. ● ● ● v7 14.6 Protect Information through Access Control Lists Protect all information stored on systems with file system, network share, claims, application, or database specific access control lists. These controls will enforce the principle that only authorized individuals should have access to the information based on their need to access the information as a part of their responsibilities. ● ● ●

first_opinion.response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:** 
- The control can be fully verified via Azure Resource Manager APIs/CLI/SDK/PowerShell.
- The audit mentions the necessary Azure CLI command to check and remediate the public network access setting.
- The public network access property is a standard Azure resource property.
- It does not require SSH/file system access or human judgement.

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_function_app_public_network_access_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
The automated program would check the following:

- **APIs/Commands to Use:** 
  - Azure Resource Manager API or Azure CLI:
    - `az functionapp show`

- **Resources to Enumerate:** 
  - All Function Apps within the specified resource group or subscription.

- **Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:** 
  - The property to check is `properties.publicNetworkAccess`.

- **Pass/Fail Criteria with Expected Values:**
  - Check if `publicNetworkAccess` is set to `Disabled`.
  - **Pass**: Value is `Disabled`.
  - **Fail**: Value is `Enabled` or is not set.

- **Example API Endpoint or CLI Command:**
  - Use the CLI command: 
    ```bash
    az functionapp show --name <function-app-name> --resource-group <resource-group-name> --query "properties.publicNetworkAccess"
    ```

The above command will directly query the setting for public network access, enabling automated compliance checks against this control.
